title: Samsung Electronics, SK Hynix shares tumble over 7% as chip rout spreads from Wall Street
description: Shares of Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix plummeted more than 7% in early Thursday trading.

title: Europe wants to rebalance trade with Beijing, but can't quit Chinese air conditioners
description: An historic heat wave has driven unprecedented demand for Chinese-made air conditioners in Europe, underscoring the tough task Brussels faces in rebalancing trade with Beijing.

title: Oil falls as easing Middle East tensions send Brent to its worst quarter since 2020
description: Oil prices fell more than 1% on Wednesday after talks between Washington and Tehran concluded.

title: Shares of BYD and Xiaomi surge as June delivery figures fuel optimism
description: Hong Kong-listed shares of Chinese electric vehicle makers surged, after companies posted June vehicle data that helped to buoy investor sentiment.

title: Alibaba-affiliate Ant Group rushes into humanoid robots with a dozen deals in 18 months
description: Ant has led a 500 million yuan ($73.59 million) funding round in humanoid robotics company Zeroth, the start-up announced Thursday.

title: Inside India newsletter: Amazon, Walmart-owned Flipkart get ready to shake up India's delivery-in-minutes sector
description: Amazon and Walmart-owned Flipkart are scrambling to expand quick commerce operations in India, not just to chase growth but to remain relevant to consumers.

title: ‘China’s Instagram’ targets male users as RedNote readies for IPO
description: Social media platform Xiaohongshu accelerates plans for Hong Kong listing

title: Warsh Must Beware of Curves Flattening to Deceive
description: The bond market is taking him seriously, but that’s not necessarily great news.

title: Headline's Zhu on Asia Tech Opportunities
description: Clarey Zhu, Partner at Headline Growth, discusses the investment opportunities in Asia's tech sector, including AI infrastructure, deep tech and robotics. She speaks with Haidi Stroud-Watts from the sidelines of the IVS2026 conference in Kyoto. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Citi's Luet: China's Franchise Has Grown Very Strongly
description: Marc Luet, Head of Japan, Asia North & Australia and Banking at Citi, says the bank has seen the role of the yuan grow and is hopeful of obtaining a securities license in China. He speaks exclusively on "Bloomberg: The China Show." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Japan 10-Year Bond Sale Sees Weaker Demand Than 12-Month Average
description: Japan’s 10-year government bond auction drew weaker demand than the average for the past year on concerns the Bank of Japan isn’t raising rates quickly enough to contain inflation.

title: Iran’s Floating Oil Stockpile Swells as Major Buyers Stay Away
description: A hoard of Iranian oil is building up at sea, as the Islamic Republic struggles to find buyers before the expiry of a 60-day window granted by Washington.

title: Korean Stocks Fall as Jitters Over Meta and Apple Hit Chipmakers
description: South Korean stocks slumped as Meta Platforms Inc.’s plan to sell computing power raised questions over excess in AI capacity, driving a selloff in chipmakers.

title: CATL Says Mining, Not Refining, Is Battery Making’s Big Hurdle
description: Securing supplies of key raw materials has become a priority for the world’s largest manufacturer of electric vehicle batteries.

title: Iron Ore Advances as CMRG May Block Some Fortescue Deliveries
description: Iron ore advanced — with futures briefly topping $100 a ton — after China’s state-backed buyer of the commodity signaled that it planned to restrict some Fortescue Ltd. inventories held at mainland ports.

title: US Tech Rout Sours Mood For Japan, South Korean Chip Stocks
description: Japanese and South Korean semiconductor stocks tumbled Thursday after a selloff in US technology shares soured the mood across Asia. Concerns over greater competition increased after Apple Inc. was said to be in negotiations to buy Chinese chips.

title: India IPO Pipeline Hits Record as Big Guns Line Up
description: Bankers expect IPO activity to accelerate in the coming months.

title: Global Appetite for Korea Grows Ahead of 24-Hour Won Trading
description: Global investors’ interest in South Korea’s economy and capital markets has “clearly changed” for the better in recent years, the country’s top finance ministry official said, as the won prepares to shift to round-the-clock trading on July 6.

title: Data Center Firm Switch Seeks $2 Billion in Funding Round
description: Switch Inc. is kicking off a private funding round led by Andreessen Horowitz that could raise about $2 billion, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Gold Extends Gain After Warsh Remarks Ease Rate-Hike Prospects
description: Gold extended a rebound after a speech by US Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh dampened speculation the central bank may hike interest rates this year to tackle inflation.

title: Mirae Says a Lead Underwriter Confirmed Its SpaceX IPO Orders
description: Mirae Asset Securities Co. said it carried out all its work related to SpaceX’s initial public offering in coordination with the lead underwriters on the deal, disputing a Bloomberg News report published June 30.

title: Takaichi to Boost India Ties as China Tensions Rise
description: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi arrived in India for a three-day trip to deepen economic ties and strengthen security cooperation with counterpart Narendra Modi. Bloomberg's Yoshiaki Nohara breaks down the context. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: SEC Probes Alleged Insider Trades That Cost Susquehanna
description: The SEC is looking into Susquehanna International Group’s allegations that unknown insider traders made $100 million on options bets ahead of a recent Chinese regulatory crackdown on cross-border brokerages, according to a person familiar with the matter.

title: Emerging Asia Bonds Draw Global Funds Despite Fed Hike Fears
description: Foreign investors are piling back into Asian emerging-market bonds despite renewed Federal Reserve hawkishness, as expectations that regional central banks will keep interest rates elevated support the debt’s yield appeal.

title: At least eight killed in major missile and drone strikes on Kyiv
description: Russia launched its biggest strikes on the city in weeks

title: Aunt of Venezuelan boy pulled from rubble tells BBC she will give him 'mother's warmth'
description: Kleiber Moran, 2, was rescued on Wednesday, six days after last week's deadly earthquakes.

title: Did this AI anti-drug video make drugs look appealing?
description: Hong Kong’s Correctional Services Department removed a AI-generated K-pop video, after facing backlash that it made substances look too appealing.

title: U.S. Resumes Dollar Transfers to Iraq After Monthslong Suspension
description: The Trump administration had halted the shipments to Iraq as part of its efforts to pressure the Baghdad government to distance itself from Iran.

title: Can the World’s Largest Stadium Explain the Global Economy?
description: High growth rates and surging exports in Vietnam mask deep frustrations in one of Asia’s most ambitious countries.

title: Can Companies Embrace A.I. Without Layoffs? This One Says It Is Trying to.
description: The German software giant SAP says it is betting that employees can reinvent jobs instead of eliminating them. Experts are divided on whether it will work.

title: For Congo Fans, the Thrill of Victory Was So Close, Until It Wasn’t
description: Congo lost 2-1 to England on Wednesday night, after spending most of the game winning.

title: Russia Strikes Ukraine as Explosions Rock Capital of Kyiv
description: At least 10 people were killed in the assault, which began hours after President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine warned that Russia was preparing another “massive strike” on Kyiv.

title: German prosecutors charge Ukrainian suspect over Nord Stream explosions
description: Prosecutors allege a yacht was used in the sabotage of pipelines, with the suspect leading the operation.

title: Lawyer takes Trump to Task over Unchecked Presidential Powers
description: Constitutional lawyer Bruce Fein says the US was founded on the principle that governments exist to protect rights.

title: EU border rules causing travel chaos ahead of summer peak, industry warns
description: European airlines and airports call for flexibility to suspend digital border system amid severe delays.

title: On the front line of Congo’s Ebola outbreak
description: Al Jazeera's Catherine Wambua-Soi reports from Bunia, on the front line of the Ebola response.

title: USA beat Bosnia 2-0 despite Balogun red card, enter World Cup last 16
description: Balogun scored USA's first goal and was sent off for a foul, but the home team scored another to advance to next round.

title: At least 8 killed in Kyiv after Zelenskyy warns of ‘massive Russian strike’
description: At least eight dead and dozens injured as Ukraine's forces defend against ballistic missiles, drones targeting capital.

title: Ocean temperatures hit record highs as El Niño looms
description: The world’s oceans are under heat stress, with average sea surface temperatures hitting 21°C.

title: ‘A war zone’: Venezuela aid workers fear health crisis after earthquakes
description: Aid workers fear disease outbreaks in temporary shelters amid overcrowding, lack of access to clean water, sanitation.

title: Belgium stage 3–2 comeback win over Senegal to enter World Cup last 16
description: Tielemans's penalty late into stoppage time of extra time capped Belgium's comeback from 2-0 down in Seattle.

title: Iran war live: ‘Positive progress’ as US, Tehran wrap up indirect talks
description: Tehran says a 'communication channel' will be established with Washington to report and discuss breaches of the MoU.

title: From AI chips to energy drinks: Hong Kong’s biggest listing deals of 2026 so far
description: Hong Kong’s stock exchange ranked second globally by capital raised in the first half of 2026, with total proceeds from initial public offerings and secondary listings rising 84.3 per cent year on year to US$26.4 billion. Among the 84 new listings during the period, mainland Chinese issuers accounted for 98.5 per cent of the total, with the majority concentrated in advanced technology and consumer staples. Below are the five largest deals: 1. Victory Giant Technology, US$2.73 billion The...

title: PDD embraces China’s ‘city of the future’ after bruising regulatory clash and record fine
description: The operator of budget online marketplaces Temu and Pinduoduo is ramping up its commitment in a state-backed development zone – dubbed a “city of the future” in official discourse – months after the e-commerce group was hit by the heaviest penalty in a record food-safety crackdown and accused by regulators of obstructing enforcement. PDD Holdings had more than 600 employees at its unit in the Xiongan New Area by the end of June, making it the development zone’s largest privately owned internet...

title: China’s tax crackdown shakes A-share and Hong Kong-listed firms with major payouts
description: China’s anti-tax evasion campaign is sweeping across the A-share and Hong Kong stock markets, with at least 80 listed firms already having been ordered to pay back corporate income taxes and late fees to local authorities. The number of listed companies involved in the first half of the year is likely to soon surpass the total of 89 for full-year 2025, raising concerns that Beijing’s taxation drive could be extended amid local debt black holes, which would threaten corporate earnings, cash flows...

title: Can Hong Kong absorb US$100 billion of newly tradable shares amid the global AI boom?
description: Hong Kong stocks have trailed other major global markets in the first half of the year, a trend analysts said could persist as sluggish consumer spending weighs on Chinese internet platforms and an entrenched AI trade risks widening the performance gap. A glut of stocks from the expiry of IPO lock-up periods could pose a further threat this quarter, prompting the city’s exchange to respond quickly with countervailing measures. These included lowering trading barriers for individual investors, a...

title: 5 Under-the-Radar Stocks Fueling the AI Revolution
description: Nvidia was the first poster child of the AI revolution. OpenAI turned ChatGPT into a global phenomenon. Google, Microsoft, and Anthropic have since dominated headlines with ever-more-powerful models. But throughout history, the biggest fortunes made during a technological revolution rarely went to the most famous names. The California Gold Rush didn't create the most wealth for gold miners. It created fortunes for the people selling picks, shovels, railroads, and infrastructure. The same dynamic is emerging in AI. The future won't be decided by…

title: Why Japanese airlines' $400 fuel surcharges are hitting now — and when they will end
description: Fuel surcharges at Japan Airlines and All Nippon Airways increased Wednesday, despite falling jet fuel costs in Asia.

title: South Korean government discriminated against Coupang, U.S. companies, House report finds
description: The House Judiciary Committee said the South Korean government discriminated against Coupang and other U.S. companies, in a new report.

title: Protein-packed Cheerios and cat food: How General Mills plans to combat a tough spending backdrop
description: “Cat growth is on fire,” one executive said.

title: Opening a ‘Trump account’ for your children? Here is the risk you need to reckon with first.
description: The new accounts ban bonds and international stocks, forcing parents to bet their children’s financial future entirely on U.S. equities.

title: Hamilton Lane Targets $220 Million for First China Yuan Fund
description: Hamilton Lane Inc., a private market investor that manages $1 trillion, is planning its first yuan-denominated fund to target discounted Chinese assets, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: KKR-Backed Musinsa Eyes 'Fast Growth' in China
description: South Korean fashion powerhouse Musinsa is rolling out an aggressive physical store expansion in China and Japan, Co-CEO Nam Cho tells Bloomberg's Shery Anh in an exclusive interview. The firm is also looking at domestic and international exchanges for a highly-anticipated IPO. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Apple is reportedly planning new iPad Pro and MacBook Pro releases early next year
